C1.emotional_beat: A cold snap of certainty: this wasn’t a bad fall—Ellison was deprived of oxygen first, and the ‘accident’ story collapses into something planned.

detective_annotations.content: Hypoxia BEFORE impact — this flips A1’s ‘accident’ assumption.
detective_annotations.style_hint: yellow digital highlight with a tight callout box

detective_annotations.content: Time window ~23:10–23:30. Compare to B6 last activity + any system events.
Bruising on both upper arms = handled/dragged? Staging after collapse.

C1.reading_order_note: Best read immediately before C2 so the player is primed to interpret ‘maintenance/silence’ and purge events as a homicide method, not a safety glitch.

C2.emotional_beat: An exacting, stomach-dropping aha: the timeline is engineered—silenced monitor, purge event, then a tidy export—each timestamp tightening the trap.

detective_annotations.content: 22:48 MAINT/SILENCE → paging disabled. This is the answer to ‘my phone never rang’ (B3).
detective_annotations.style_hint: cyan highlight with a rectangular callout

detective_annotations.content: 23:12 purge → O2 drop → aligns with C1 window. Method, not accident.
Could ‘MAINT’ be a routine nightly test? If so, why paired with a purge + late export?
DR node / checksum = harder to tamper with. Treat as ‘source of truth.’

C2.reading_order_note: Read before C3: C2 establishes the raw, append-only ‘what happened’ record; C3 then shows the polished narrative someone tried to publish on top of it.

C3.emotional_beat: Rage and disbelief: someone generated a clean, regulator-friendly ‘sensor fault’ story minutes after the dangerous window—before anyone ‘found’ the body.

detective_annotations.content: Generated at 23:36… same night. Why writing the story BEFORE discovery at 06:14 (A1)?
detective_annotations.style_hint: pink highlight with a bold timestamp callout

detective_annotations.content: ‘Transient sensor fault’ — but C2 shows a purge + O2 drop. This reads like damage control.
detective_annotations.style_hint: red circle with a short side-note

detective_annotations.content: Generated by account label ≠ person. Confirm who ‘RPATEL (QA-DIR)’ maps to + whether it’s shared.

C3.reading_order_note: Works best as the last C doc: after the player trusts C2’s raw log, this ‘print to PDF’ summary feels like a deliberate attempt to overwrite reality.
